zone_summary: Feature-flagged cursor pagination for listDAGExecutionIterationIds, including continuation-header contract, optional/default page sizing, filter-bound tokens, stable global ordering, and bounded deep scans.

behavioral_changes.summary: The controller now treats historyCount as optional under FLTListDagAPIPagination and sources missing values from the new DefaultHistoryCount workload parameter.
Effective historyCount is clamped to 500, and pagination-disabled requests null out any supplied continuationToken before querying storage.
Successful paginated calls expose the next-page cursor in the x-ms-continuation-token header, and the Swagger contract now advertises that response header.
Continuation tokens are decoded from reverseEpoch/IterationId/filterHash cursors; malformed tokens and filter changes now raise ArgumentException that the controller maps to HTTP 400.
Storage reads now page after a cursor with SDK continuation support, counting only status-matching files so sparse status filters can still produce full pages.
Deep cursor scans are bounded to 200 storage batches and fail fast with a user-facing 'narrow your search criteria' message instead of scanning indefinitely.
Aggregated results are globally sorted by StartedAt then IterationId, cursor-filtered after the global sort, and use an N+1 extra record to decide whether to mint the next continuation token.
Filter hashes normalize status and MLV-ID ordering so logically equivalent filter sets continue the same pagination stream.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the host-configured default page size used when historyCount is omitted and pagination is enabled.
Makes historyCount optional and resolves the effective page size from the new workload parameter when the feature flag is on.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: if (!isPaginationEnabled) { continuationToken = null; } ... effectiveHistoryCount = Math.Clamp(effectiveHistoryCount, 1, MaxHistoryCount);
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the flag-off compatibility path and the new upper-bound clamp for requested page size.
Adds the observable HTTP response header that carries the next-page cursor.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: "x-ms-continuation-token": { "description": "Continuation token for retrieving the next page of results. Absent when no more pages exist." }
grounding_evidence.reason: Extends the published API schema with the new continuation response header contract.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(...ContinuationToken)) { if (ContinuationTokenHelper.TryDecode(...)) { if (ContinuationTokenHelper.ValidateFilterHash(...)) { ... } else { throw new ArgumentException("Search criteria have changed. Please refresh to see updated results."); } } else { throw new ArgumentException("Page data is no longer available. Please refresh to reload."); } }
grounding_evidence.reason: Implements continuation-token parsing plus explicit failures for stale-filter and malformed-token cases.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: catch (ArgumentException ex) { ... resultCode = HttpStatusCode.BadRequest; return CreateErrorResponse(ex.Message, resultCode); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Maps the new token-validation failures to HTTP 400 instead of letting them bubble as generic server errors.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: bool hasStatusFilter = listDagExecutionIterationsRequestFilters.Statuses?.Count > 0; int fetchLimit = hasStatusFilter ? MaxFetchPerFolder : historyCount + 1;
grounding_evidence.reason: First-page listing now over-fetches when status filters are present so sparse matches can still fill a page.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: var (batchPaths, nextToken) = await fs.ListWithContinuationAsync(folderPath, MaxFetchPerFolder, storageContinuationToken, cancellationToken); ... if (hasStatusFilter && !statusSet.Contains(status)) { continue; } ... if (matchingCount >= neededCount) { return filesAfterCursor; }
grounding_evidence.reason: Subsequent-page scans now use paged storage listing and count only matching statuses toward the next page threshold.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: if (++batchCount > MaxCursorScanBatches) { throw new InvalidOperationException("Too many records to search through. Please narrow your search criteria and try again."); }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the defensive cap that stops deep cursor scans after 200 storage batches.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: var sortedIterations = dagExecutionIterations.OrderByDescending(d => d.StartedAt).ThenByDescending(d => d.IterationId.ToString()).ToList(); ... var itemsWithExtra = filteredIterations.Take(historyCount + 1).ToList(); ... nextContinuationToken = ContinuationTokenHelper.Encode(lastItem.StartedAt.Value, lastItem.IterationId, filterHash);
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the global ordering, page slicing, and next-token generation semantics for pagination.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: string rawToken = string.Join(Separator.ToString(), reverseEpoch, iterationId, filterHash ?? string.Empty);
grounding_evidence.reason: The token payload now carries both reverseEpoch and IterationId, enabling deterministic page boundaries for same-timestamp items.
Normalizes collection-valued filters before hashing so equivalent filter sets produce the same continuation-token hash.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: await foreach (Page<PathItem> page in pageable.AsPages(continuationToken: ..., pageSizeHint: effectiveMaxCount)) { ... nextContinuationToken = page.ContinuationToken; if (paths.Count >= effectiveMaxCount) { break; } }
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds resumable SDK-backed listing support that the pagination scan depends on for deep folders.

scenario_sketches.title: Paginating across multiple folders returns globally descending, non-overlapping pages
scenario_sketches.rationale: Create interleaved execution histories across multiple Index_StartTime_* folders, walk page 1/page 2/... with continuation tokens, and assert the combined traversal returns each item exactly once in global StartedAt-descending order.

scenario_sketches.title: Responses emit x-ms-continuation-token only when more pages exist, and omit it on the last page
scenario_sketches.rationale: Validate the new response-header contract by asserting a continuation header on a non-terminal page, no header on the final page, and Swagger exposure of the header name/description.

scenario_sketches.title: Omitted historyCount uses DefaultHistoryCount when pagination is enabled
scenario_sketches.rationale: Stub the parameters provider to a recognizable value and verify that an enabled request with no historyCount passes that workload-configured page size downstream.

scenario_sketches.title: historyCount above 500 is clamped before downstream pagination work
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise below-cap, at-cap, and above-cap values so the test locks in the new 500 maximum, especially the observable 1000→500 case.

scenario_sketches.title: With FLTListDagAPIPagination off, continuationToken is ignored and no continuation header is returned
scenario_sketches.rationale: Hold the handler/store capable of returning a next token, disable the feature flag, and verify the controller clears the incoming token and suppresses the outgoing header for backward compatibility.

scenario_sketches.title: Malformed continuation tokens return 400 with the reload guidance message
scenario_sketches.rationale: Pass an undecodable token and assert the persistence layer rejects it with the reload message that the controller surfaces as BadRequest.

scenario_sketches.title: Changing filters between pages returns 400 with the search-criteria-changed message
scenario_sketches.rationale: Fetch a first page, then reuse its token while changing status/time/MLV filters and verify the filter-hash mismatch path returns the explicit refresh message as HTTP 400.

scenario_sketches.title: Equivalent status and MLV filters in different query orders keep the same continuation-token hash
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use the same logical filter sets in different list orders and prove the helper/controller treat them as equivalent rather than spuriously rejecting the next page.

scenario_sketches.title: Sparse status filters still fill pages and emit a next token
scenario_sketches.rationale: Arrange many non-matching files with only occasional matching statuses so the first-page and after-cursor fetch paths must over-scan to return historyCount matching items and a continuation token.

scenario_sketches.title: Same-timestamp iterations page by IterationId descending without duplicates across the page boundary
scenario_sketches.rationale: Place multiple iterations with identical StartedAt around the cutoff so the test proves the IterationId tie-breaker controls both order and the next-page cursor boundary.

scenario_sketches.title: Cursor scanning aborts after 200 storage batches with the narrow-search-criteria failure
scenario_sketches.rationale: Mock repeated low-yield or empty paged listings with continuation tokens and assert the defensive batch cap throws the explicit narrowing guidance instead of scanning unboundedly.
